title: Binary Search — ROI & Pattern
oneSentence: Binary search divides the search interval in half recursively in sorted data.
description: Master the standard sorted-array Binary Search template twice. First through guided line-by-line tracing, then by reconstructing the core steps.

roi.complexity: Time: O(log N) | Space: O(1) in-place
roi.signals: Target search in sorted data, find first/last occurrence, optimization search.

codeLines.text: public static int search(int[] nums, int target) {
// STEP 2 — LOOP: Keep searching while bounds are valid
// STEP 3 — MID: Calculate middle element (prevent overflow)
// STEP 4 — CHECK: Evaluate mid and prune half of search space

traceSteps.title: 1. Search Space Init
traceSteps.text: Set low index to 0 and high index to the last array element.

traceSteps.title: 2. Search Space Loop
traceSteps.text: Continue searching while search space bounds are valid (low <= high).

traceSteps.title: 3. Midpoint Calculation
traceSteps.text: Compute middle index using low + (high - low) / 2 to avoid integer overflow.

traceSteps.title: 4. Target Found Match
traceSteps.text: If mid matches target, return the middle index.

traceSteps.title: 5. Search Right Half
traceSteps.text: If mid value is too small, target must lie in the right half (low = mid + 1).

traceSteps.title: 6. Search Left Half
traceSteps.text: If mid value is too large, target must lie in the left half (high = mid - 1).

traceSteps.title: 7. Search Fail Fallback
traceSteps.text: Return -1 if target is not found in the array.

sandbox.question: How do you calculate mid to prevent integer overflow?
What is the correct loop condition to ensure all elements are checked?
If nums[mid] is smaller than target, where is the target?
